physicalDetails.detail: Young human woman roughly Jaina's age, dark hair pulled back hard, robes cut on Dalaran lines but dyed in Barov burgundy, with ink on one thumb and reagent scars between the fingers of her right hand.

Her body reads as scholar before noblewoman: sleeves designed not to catch at the wrist, hands marked by laboratory work, and a quickness of gaze that makes the resemblance to Jaina feel immediate rather than flattering.

Chapter 12 gives only a threshold glimpse, but it sharpens her into controlled pride: one hand on the sanctum door, chin angled with excitement, fingers tightening on the latch when she starts to overexplain Jaina's progress to her master.

Chapter 13 puts Jandice back on page as living scholarly volatility: Barov burgundy, reagent-burned hands, chalk dust in the sleeve seam, and the bruised urgency of someone who is angrier about exclusion than afraid of an intruder.

voiceMarkers.detail: Begins in curated noble-host mode, speaking in polished, controlled sentences that present Scholomance as serious scholarship rather than atrocity.
The moment her pride is engaged, the voice speeds up, sharpens, and spills into true technical enthusiasm. She wants recognition more than reassurance.
In chapter 12 her voice only flashes for a line, but it is enough: she reports Jaina's progress upward with eager precision, sounding pleased that the master's guest proved as quick as she had hoped.
Her chapter-13 voice is bitterness sharpened into useful detail. She tells the truth through insult, making every answer about Jaina and Kel'Thuzad sound half like report and half like grievance.

beliefs.belief: Scholomance's work is rigorous, honest scholarship unfairly misnamed by people too squeamish or timid to follow the method where it leads.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Active and unchallenged for her inside chapter 10, though the chapter frames the belief as morally catastrophic.

beliefs.belief: Recognition from the right peer matters more than ordinary social approval; if Jaina sees the quality of the work, that recognition proves something real.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the way Jandice keeps overexplaining whenever Jaina shows genuine comprehension.

beliefs.belief: Kel'Thuzad's recognition remains the final credential that matters inside Scholomance, even after Jaina's peer recognition opens the way down.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 12 when Jandice immediately yields the room and the argument once Kel'Thuzad dismisses her.

beliefs.belief: Being left out of the real conversation is an injury to status as much as a security decision, and she cannot resist making that injury audible.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the way she frames Jaina and the study below for Sylvanas.

beliefs.belief: The upper filth and stock rooms are ordinary parts of the institution, not secrets worth moral recoil from inside her own worldview.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by how casually she treats the workrooms Sylvanas has just crossed.

goalsHistory.active: Complete the handoff cleanly and stay useful by not overstepping once Kel'Thuzad takes the conversation for himself.
goalsHistory.underlying: Keep credit for recognizing Jaina's value without challenging the master's control of the deeper exchange.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Stay alive, get clear of the upper levels, and leave the sanctum damage for other people to pay first.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Preserve herself after being humiliated both by Kel'Thuzad's exclusion and by Sylvanas's grip.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Proud excitement checked into obedience. Kel'Thuzad's brief approval warms her, but the dismissal reminds her exactly where her authority stops.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Furious, stung, and practical enough to run the instant attention turns elsewhere.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Takes over the interview from Alexei the moment the problem becomes scholarly instead of political.
Rows Jaina across Darrowmere herself, choosing direct escort rather than handing her off to servants.
Shows Jaina the lecture rooms, archive, and selected research logs because she wants the work seen by someone qualified to appreciate it.
Explains resonance as the organizing concept behind the missing trigger problem and then seeks permission to take Jaina lower.
Delivers Jaina to the sanctum threshold and immediately advertises the guest's quickness to Kel'Thuzad, still unable to resist proving that she chose well.
Leaves the room the moment Kel'Thuzad dismisses her, obeying the institutional ceiling she had already taught Jaina to notice.
Attempts to stop Sylvanas with magic in the upper levels and fails.
Gives up the study below, the old crypt stair, and the basic truth that Jaina was being kept there because she could keep up.
Cuts herself free and leaves before the ascent brings the others back past the cabinet.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Can stage-manage a highly controlled intellectual tour without losing command of the room's surface tone.
Matches Jaina at peer speed on theory and notation, proving genuine high-level magical education rather than ornamental dabbling.
Uses vanity, access, and selective disclosure as a form of soft control even while believing she is simply being honest about quality.
Can present another scholar's progress upward to authority in a way that mixes technical precision with personal investment, turning report into bid for recognition.
Fast enough with a spell and focus ring to threaten an armed intruder until Sylvanas closes the distance.
Resourceful enough to escape restraint and clear the upper levels while the sanctum fight holds everyone else below.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Alexei Barov
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses her given name with paternal impatience, as if this exact handoff from politics to scholarship is familiar.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Jaina Proudmoore
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Begins formal, then settles quickly into 'Jandice' once the conversation becomes technical.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Kel'Thuzad
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Addresses her as 'Mistress Barov,' a formality that acknowledges competence while keeping hierarchy intact.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: Starts with 'Lady Proudmoore' and slides into a faster peer mode once Jaina proves she can actually read the work.
Refers to him as 'the master' in institutional shorthand, a title that sounds departmental rather than devotional.
Still frames her as 'Lady Proudmoore' at the threshold because the exchange has moved back under formal supervision.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Measures people with her eyes the way Jaina does, then stills her hands a half-second too late whenever the theory genuinely excites her.
Carries herself with practiced social composure, but the posture leans forward when she thinks she is about to be understood.
At the sanctum threshold she tries to hold composure and pride at once: hand on the door, chin high, excitement visible until Kel'Thuzad's interruption cuts it short.
Pain tightens her face before fear does, and even pinned against the wall she keeps trying to win status back through what she knows.

knowledge.detail: Knows the visible Scholomance curriculum, ward placements, public archive, and the limits of her own access beneath the curated route.
Knows Jaina is academically significant enough to be shown the work and dangerous enough to require permission for anything deeper.
Understands the resonance theory behind the plague's host-conditioning problem, though chapter 10 stops short of confirming she possesses the complete final model herself.
Knows enough about the sanctum hierarchy to hand Jaina over and withdraw at once once Kel'Thuzad claims the room.
Knows the fast covert route to the sanctum runs down the old crypt spine past the hidden workrooms rather than along the guest route.
Also knows Jaina and Kel'Thuzad spent enough time in real two-way exchange for jealousy to turn into a poisoned but accurate read.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Chapter 12 keeps Jandice at the threshold and proves that threshold is exactly what she is. She is still Jaina's near mirror, but now the file adds the sharper truth that her brilliance operates inside a hierarchy she has fully accepted, even when recognition makes her forget herself for a beat.

arc.lie: If the method is rigorous enough, the moral question is secondary or already settled by the quality of the work.
arc.truth: Not accepted by her on page; chapter 10 instead frames Jandice as the threshold version of Jaina who chose scholarship without ethical brake.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter 13 shifts Jandice from threshold guide to live loose end. Her need to be understood still opens doors, but now it also leaves her wounded, useful, and at large.
